_comment: Example KiteWrt routing preset — illustrates the rules format. Policy: keep a few example domains and your home country direct, and route everything else through the VPN (route.final = the selector). KiteWrt prepends its own baseline (sniff + DNS hijack + private->direct), so these rules run after that. Name-based rules here are auto-mirrored into the DNS block. Replace the placeholder domains and the geoip country with your own. Ships no data of its own — the geoip rule-set is downloaded by sing-box at runtime through the VPN. If you rely on region-specific GeoDNS, set Direct DNS (Settings -> DNS) to a resolver in that region.

rules._comment: Example: keep specific domains off the VPN (home services, your bank, a regional site, ...). Replace with your own. `domain` matches the apex exactly; `domain_suffix` (with the leading dot) matches subdomains.
Example: send a domain through the VPN (place such overrides BEFORE any broad direct rule so they win). Replace with your own.
Example: keep your home country direct via a geoip rule-set (for IP-only connections with no domain to match).

rule_set._comment: sing-box downloads this at runtime; download_detour 'proxy' fetches it through the VPN (KiteWrt rewrites it to the selector). Point url at your country's geoip set — see github.com/SagerNet/sing-geoip (geoip-de, geoip-gb, geoip-us, ...).
